target_setter
  • Target Setter Documentation
  • Edit on GitHub

Target Setter Documentation¶

Welcome to the official documentation for Target Setter. This documentation provides system overview, installation instructions, technical references, and theoretical background used in the project.

Core Documentation¶

  • Introduction
    • Background
    • Objective
    • Scope of work
  • Hardware Specification
    • Robot platform
    • Sensors
    • Drive system
    • Sensors
    • Compute hardware
    • Network infrastructure
    • Power system
    • Safety constraints
    • Notes
  • Installation and Configuration
    • Prerequisites
    • Workspace Setup
    • Configuration
    • Running the Application
    • Common Setup Pitfalls
  • Technical Reference
    • Architecture
    • Waypoint queue and state machine
    • State machine
    • Communication protocol
    • Coordinate convention and control logic
    • Control logic
    • Path planning
    • Odometry handling
    • Inverse Kinematic
  • Troubleshooting Guide
    • Communication issues
    • Motor/driver issues
    • Sensor issues
    • App issues
    • Safety/emergency stop
    • PD controller/motion control
    • Low-level hardware checks

Build a responsive ui with constraintlayout. Accessed: 2026-02-13. URL: https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/views/layout/constraint-layout.

Coordinates and transformations. Accessed: 2026-02-15. URL: https://motion.cs.illinois.edu/RoboticSystems/CoordinateTransformations.html.

Mvc framework – architecture. Accessed: 2026-02-13. URL: https://www.tutorialspoint.com/mvc_framework/mvc_framework_architecture.htm.

Model-view-viewmodel (mvvm). Accessed: 2026-02-13. URL: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/architecture/maui/mvvm.

[[Ref]5]

Ros 2: what is dds? Accessed: 2026-02-15. URL: https://community.rti.com/page/ros-2-what-dds.

[[Ref]6]

Interfaces. 2026. ROS 2 Documentation: Humble. URL: http://docs.ros.org/en/humble/Concepts/Basic/About-Interfaces.html.

Topics. 2026. ROS 2 Documentation: Humble. URL: https://docs.ros.org/en/humble/Concepts/Basic/About-Topics.html.

A. Boghossian, J. Brown, and S. Zak. P, i, d, pi, pd, and pid control. Accessed: 2026-02-16. URL: https://eng.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/Industrial_and_Systems_Engineering/Chemical_Process_Dynamics_and_Controls_(Woolf)/09:_Proportional-Integral-Derivative_(PID)_Control/9.02:_P_I_D_PI_PD_and_PID_control.

B. P. Lathi. Principles of Linear Systems and Signals. Oxford University Press, 2 edition, 2009.

[[Ref]15]

E. A. Lee and P. P. Varaiya. Structure and Interpretation of Signals and Systems. Pearson Education, 2003.

[[Ref]16]

S. Lipschutz and M. L. Lipson. Schaum’s Outline of Discrete Mathematics. McGraw-Hill, 3 edition, 2007.

[[Ref]17]

A. Nandwanya. Constraintlayout, demystified – how it really works and when you should reach for it. September 2025. Accessed: 2026-02-12. URL: https://androidengineers.substack.com/p/constraintlayout-demystified-how.

S. Narayana. Cs 352 user datagram protocol. Accessed: 2026-02-14. URL: https://people.cs.rutgers.edu/~sn624/352-S21/lectures/transport-udp-error-detection.pdf.

H. P. Oliveira and others. Dynamic models for omni-directional robots with 3 and 4 wheels. In ICINCO. 2008.

A. Phunopas and S. Inoue. Motion improvement of four-wheeled omnidirectional mobile robots for indoor terrain. Journal of Robotics, Networking and Artificial Life, 4(4):275–282, 2018.

A. Silberschatz, P. B. Galvin, and G. Gagne. Operating System Concepts. Wiley, 10 edition, 2018.

S. Simrock. Control theory. Accessed: 2026-02-16. URL: https://cds.cern.ch/record/1100534/files/p73.pdf.
